zh.lifeSummary: Amy Amis於1500年出生在英國艾可。她和John Amis至少有1個女兒。她去世於1574年在她的家鄉享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Amy Amis于1500年出生在英国艾可。她和John Amis至少有1个女儿。她去世于1574年在她的家乡享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: Amis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Ames  .

variants.name: Ames
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Amis or from the feminine form, Amice. The Old French word amis, is from Latin amicus ‘friend’, which was used in Late Latin as a term for a man of the lower classes, in particular a slave. There were also derivatives of this as personal names, in particular masculine Amicius and feminine Amicia. Both were in use as personal names in England and may have contributed to the surname.
German: perhaps a nickname for an active person, from an ancient Germanic word related to Old High German amazzig ‘busy’.
Dutch: possibly a patronymic from Ame a common personal name in Holland and Friesland, an abbreviated form of an ancient Germanic personal name, perhaps beginning with amel ‘strong’.

variants.name: Amison
meanings: English: patronymic from Amis or Ames , meaning ‘son of Amice or Ami’.

variants.name: Amos
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Amis  , assimilated in spelling to the Biblical name (see 2 below). It occurs chiefly in southeastern England.

Jewish, German, English, Scottish, and Welsh: from the Hebrew personal name Amos, of uncertain origin, in some traditions connected with the Hebrew verb amos ‘to carry’, and assigned the meaning ‘borne by God’. This was the name of a Biblical prophet of the 8th century BC , whose oracles are recorded in the Book of Amos. This was one of the Biblical names taken up by Puritans and Nonconformists in the 16th–17th centuries, too late to have had much influence on surname formationm in Britain, except in Wales.

nameMeanings.name: Amy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimie
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimée
meanings: originally a vernacular nickname meaning ‘beloved’, from the past participle of French aimer ‘to love’ (Latin amare; compare Amy ). It has been in use in French since the Middle Ages, although it has never been very common. It is now also used, with or without the accent, as a given name in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Amie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Amy  , corresponding in form to the French vocabulary word meaning ‘(female) friend’.

variants.name: Ami
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: Amy Amis was born in 1500, in Essex, England. She had at least 1 daughter with John Amis. She died in 1574, in her hometown, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Amy Amis est née en 1500, à Essex, Angleterre. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec John Amis. Elle est décédée en 1574, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Amy Amis wurde 1500 in Essex, England geboren. Sie hatte mit John Amis mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1574 im Alter von 74 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Amy Amis è nata il nel 1500 nel Essex, England. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con John Amis. Morì nel 1574, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 74 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Amy Amisは1500年にイギリスエセックスで生まれた。彼女はJohn Amisとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1574年に故郷で74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Amy Amis(은)는 1500년 영국 에세스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 John Amis(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1574년 그녀의 고향에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Amy Amis nasceu em 1500, em Essex, Inglaterra. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com John Amis. Ela faleceu em 1574, em sua cidade natal, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Amy Amis родилась в 1500 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от John Amis. Она умерла в 1574 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 74 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Amy Amis nació en 1500, en Essex, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con John Amis. Murió en 1574, en su pueblo, a la edad de 74 años.
